title: torch-npu 2.5.x requires pyyaml as runtime transitive dependency not declared in pip metadata
problem_description: torch-npu 2.5.1's torch_npu/npu/_memory_viz.py imports yaml at module load time, but pyyaml is not declared as a dependency in the wheel's metadata. This causes ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml' when importing torch_npu, even though pip install reports no dependency issues. The error occurs during torch's device backend loading (torch._import_device_backends), making it appear as a torch core failure rather than a torch_npu dependency gap.
rough_fix_approach: 1. After installing torch-npu in a new venv, immediately run: python -c 'import torch_npu' to detect import failures before running the project entry script. 2. If ModuleNotFoundError: yaml appears, install pyyaml. 3. Proactively add pyyaml>=6.0 to the venv dependency list whenever torch-npu is installed. 4. This should be part of the standard venv creation checklist for NPU projects.
artifact_evidence: raw/phase_5_validation_attempt0.json (import failure trace showing yaml as root cause)
reasoning: This is a packaging bug in torch-npu that affects every installation. Since the dependency is not declared, pip will never auto-install it. Every future migration creating a new venv will hit this unless pyyaml is proactively added. Marked as 'document' rather than 'skill' because this is a known packaging gap that should be documented in environment setup runbooks, not something requiring complex reasoning to fix.
